InAmigos Foundation · Est. 2010

Friends who show up — that's what amigos do.

We work alongside families in underserved communities across India — in classrooms, kitchens, clinics and self-help groups — so that opportunity isn't a matter of luck.

Donate now Become a volunteer Our mission →

Who we are

A small team that grew the way good neighbourhoods do — one introduction at a time.

InAmigos began in a single classroom in a resettlement colony on the edge of Chennai, where two teachers noticed the same six children skipping school to help at home. What started as an after-hours tuition group is now a network of community centres, health camps and livelihood programs run largely by people from the neighbourhoods we work in.

Our mission is simple to say and hard to do: meet families where they are, listen longer than feels efficient, and build programs that the community can eventually run without us.

Dignity first

We design every program so people are participants, not recipients.

Local leadership

Over 70% of our field staff live in the communities they serve.

Open books

Annual reports, audits and program budgets are public by default.

Built to hand over

Every project has an exit plan from day one.

How we got here

Our story so far

2010

One room, six students

A volunteer-run evening tuition class begins in Perumbakkam.

2014

First community centre

InAmigos registers as a foundation and opens its first permanent learning centre.

2018

Health & nutrition program launches

Mobile health camps begin in partnership with local clinics across Tamil Nadu.

2021

Women's livelihood collectives

Self-help groups across 40 villages begin small-scale enterprise training.

2025

118 communities, 9 states

Programs now run in education, healthcare, nutrition, environment and skilling.

Leadership

The people steering the work

Meera Raghavan

Founder & Executive Director

Started the first tuition class in 2010; now oversees program strategy across all states.

Arjun Nair

Director of Field Programs

Leads the health and nutrition camps and trains new community coordinators.

Lakshmi Iyer

Head of Partnerships

Connects InAmigos with donors, corporates and government schemes.

What we do

Eight programs, one approach: show up consistently.

Each program is run by a local team and reviewed twice a year against its own goals.

Education

After-school learning centres, scholarships and digital literacy for children in grades 1–10.

₹14.4L raisedGoal ₹20L
Read more

Healthcare

Mobile health camps, maternal care support and referral networks with local clinics.

₹9.3L raisedGoal ₹16L
Read more

Women empowerment

Self-help groups, enterprise training and micro-loans for women-led small businesses.

₹11.8L raisedGoal ₹14L
Read more

Food distribution

Weekly ration support and mid-day meals for families facing food insecurity.

₹7.1L raisedGoal ₹11L
Read more

Environmental awareness

Tree-planting drives, waste segregation training and water conservation workshops.

₹2.8L raisedGoal ₹7L
Read more

Skill development

Vocational training in tailoring, computer literacy and trades for young adults.

₹4.5L raisedGoal ₹9L
Read more

Child welfare

Safe spaces, counselling support and protection services for at-risk children.

₹6.0L raisedGoal ₹10L
Read more

Community development

Sanitation, clean water access and small infrastructure projects led by village councils.

₹5.2L raisedGoal ₹12L
Read more

Your donation, in real terms

₹500 provides school supplies for one child for a full term.
Continue to secure payment
🔒 256-bit SSL secured 📄 80G tax exemption ✅ Registered NGO

Why your money goes further here

We keep overhead low by partnering with existing local institutions — schools, primary health centres and panchayats — rather than building parallel infrastructure.

  • 86 paise of every rupee goes directly to programs
  • Annual audits published publicly each March
  • Donations of ₹500+ qualify for an 80G certificate, emailed automatically
  • Monthly donors get a quarterly update from the program they support

Get involved

What it's like to volunteer with us

Most volunteers start with a single weekend visit. Here's the usual path from there.

1

Tell us what you're good at

Submit the form — teaching, healthcare, design, logistics, anything helps.

2

Orientation visit

A half-day visit to a centre near you, paired with an existing volunteer.

3

Pick your rhythm

Weekly, monthly, or project-based — we fit around your schedule, not the other way round.

4

Ongoing role

Many long-term volunteers go on to lead a program area within a year.

Join our mission

Thanks — we've got your details.

A volunteer coordinator will email you within 3 working days to set up an orientation visit.

Please enter your name.
Please enter a valid email.

Stories

Three years on, told by the people living them

Education · Thiruvallur district

"I was the first in my family to finish school."

Priya joined the after-school centre at age 9 after dropping out twice. With consistent tutoring and a small scholarship, she completed Class 12 and is now training to become a lab technician.

Before

Out of school, working part-time

After

Class 12 graduate, vocational training

Women empowerment · Kanchipuram

"Twelve of us started with one sewing machine."

Selvi's self-help group received seed funding and training in 2022. The group now runs a small tailoring unit supplying local schools with uniforms, and each member earns a stable monthly income.

Before

No independent income

After

12-member enterprise, steady orders

Healthcare · Villupuram

"The camp caught it early — that made all the difference."

Anitha's son was diagnosed with severe anaemia during a routine InAmigos health camp. Follow-up nutrition support and check-ins over six months brought him back to a healthy weight.

Before

Undiagnosed severe anaemia

After

Healthy growth, ongoing monitoring

Frequently asked questions

Yes — donations of ₹500 or more qualify for an 80G tax exemption certificate, which is emailed to you automatically after payment.

Yes. You can direct your donation to a specific program from the donation page, or leave it unrestricted so we can allocate it where it's needed most.

Yes — we work with several corporate partners on CSR-funded projects. Reach out via the form and our partnerships team will follow up.

We have remote volunteering roles in content, design, translation and fundraising support — mention this in the volunteer form's experience field.
